metadata.title: Research IDH Mutations and Epigenetic Reprogramming: How a Metabolic Error Locks Leukemia Cells
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E body text; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral
metadata.note: CLI video REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06 OUTPUT(manim) · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro. Source: cancer-biology/chapters/07-epigenetics-in-cancer-the-methylation-and-histone-code.md
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. IDH — reversing an epigenetic lock.

A single amino acid change in a metabolic enzyme produces a cancer-causing chemical that silences genes across the genome. A drug that removes that chemical reverses an epigenetic state accumulated over years. This is the clearest demonstration that cancer epigenetics is pharmacologically reversible.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- problem card: metabolic error causes epigenetic lock; drug reverses it

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: trace the complete IDH mutation causal chain and the clinical reversal evidence.

props.command:
claude "Trace the causal chain from IDH1/IDH2 mutation
to epigenetic phenotype in AML, citing primary
literature at each step. Then research the
clinical evidence for ivosidenib and enasidenib:
overall response rates from the pivotal trials,
and what cellular evidence confirms the epigenetic
reversal mechanism (not cytotoxicity) is responsible."

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) -- Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code. The script lays out the causal chain as a node list with evidence citation at each step — the chain from IDH mutation to differentiation block, made auditable.

props.code:
# idh_causal_chain.py -- Claude Code output
CHAIN = [
  {"node": "mutant IDH1/2",
   "mech": "neomorphic enzyme activity",
   "citation": "Ward 2010 Nature"},
  {"node": "2HG accumulates",
   "mech": "oncometabolite produced",
   "citation": "Dang 2010 Nature"},
  {"node": "TET2/KDM blocked",
   "mech": "competitive inhibition by 2HG",
   "citation": "Xu 2011 Science"},
  {"node": "hypermethylation",
   "mech": "demethylase failure -> marks persist",
   "citation": "Turcan 2012 Nature"},
  {"node": "differentiation locked",
   "mech": "blast arrest",
   "citation": "DiNardo 2018 NEJM"},
]
for i, step in enumerate(CHAIN):
  print(f"Step {i+1}: {step['node']}")
  print(f"  Mechanism: {step['mech']}")
  print(f"  Evidence: {step['citation']}")

beats.narration_text: The causal chain. Mutant IDH produces 2HG, the oncometabolite. 2HG blocks TET2 and the histone demethylases. Blocking the demethylases causes hypermethylation. Hypermethylation silences the differentiation genes. The blasts are locked. Five steps, each with a primary citation.
beats.visual_intent: Manim horizontal causal chain: five nodes left to right; each connected by arrows; all CRIMSON

beats.narration_text: Now ask whether the IDH model generalizes to other oncometabolites.

props.command:
claude "Does the IDH inhibitor model generalize?
Are there other oncometabolites that block
epigenetic enzymes and could similarly be
pharmacologically reversed? What is the clinical
development status of succinate dehydrogenase
or fumarate hydratase inhibitors?"

beats.narration_text: The generalization. IDH1/2 confirmed, FDA-approved. SDH mutations produce succinate — no approved inhibitor, Phase 1 only. FH mutations produce fumarate — preclinical. Same logic: an oncometabolite blocks epigenetic enzymes. Different oncometabolites, much earlier in the pipeline.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: IDH confirmed row + SDH and FH emerging rows; lighter/dotted for preclinical entries

beats.narration_text: The drug works by removing the cause of the methylation — the oncometabolite — not by reversing the methylation directly. This is why it works when generic demethylating agents fail. Azacitidine removes methylation globally. Ivosidenib removes the cause, and the cell's own demethylases restore the epigenome.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- summary card: cause removal vs symptom removal; ivosidenib vs azacitidine contrast

beats.narration_text: IDH1 and IDH2 mutation status is now standard molecular testing in AML and glioma. Verify the specific mutation variant — R132 for IDH1, R140 or R172 for IDH2 — as each has different inhibitor affinity. The variant determines which drug applies.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps: IDH1 R132 / IDH2 R140/R172; variant determines drug

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
